title: hieracium albanicum subsp. albanicum
intro: Hieracium albanicum subsp. albanicum, also known as the Alban Hawkweed, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae), characterized by its yellow flowers and leaves with a milky sap. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat wounds and skin conditions. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including antioxidant and antimicrobial activities, as well as potential anti-cancer properties. Hieracium albanicum subsp. albanicum can be prepared as an herbal infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat a range of ailments.
